tests.name: init_sh_non_interactive_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive completed; verify-install reported 51 passed and Setup Complete banner printed.

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh from project dir reports '[OK] Context7 MCP installed' and '[OK] Context7 MCP configured'.

tests.name: stop_checklist_registered
tests.details: .claude/settings.json Stop hooks reference stop-checklist.sh in framework path.

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: init.sh executed from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with '[FAIL] Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' (PR #18 confirmed).

tests.name: phase1_steps_complete
tests.details: All 5 phase1_architecture steps (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed via process-checklist.sh.

tests.name: build_loop_feature_completes
tests.details: uat-feat-1: 6 build_loop steps completed; git commit succeeded; test-gate.sh --record-feature wrote it to build-progress.json.

documentation_gaps.gap: check-gate.sh --backfill-host prints '[INFO] Inferred host other from origin URL' but does NOT persist a 'host' field in .claude/manifest.json — subsequent --preflight runs still fail with 'manifest.json missing host field'. Either the inference is not being written, or the preflight is reading the wrong key.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/check-gate.sh (--backfill-host vs --preflight); manifest.json post-call shows no 'host' top-level key

tests.name: intake_wizard_upgrade_deployment_works
tests.details: intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment {organizational|private_poc} aborts immediately with 'Error: PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found.' even when invoked from a valid project dir that DOES have PROJECT_INTAKE.md. Root cause: intake-wizard.sh sets PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)" which resolves to the framework directory, not the cwd. The PROJECT_INTAKE.md existence guard at line 1546 then checks the wrong path. This is U-G (still open from prior sweep) and it makes Section F's documented entry point completely non-functional. Workaround: skip intake-wizard and call scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational directly from the project — that path works.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: Workaround path: bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational completed with 'Upgrade Complete' banner and post-upgrade verification finished. U-E (poc_mode lookup) did not regress on this fresh project.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: phase1_architecture.steps_completed (all 5) preserved; build_loop reset cleanly (feature: null) is expected post-record.

tests.name: deployment_field_written_to_phase_state
tests.details: U-J confirmation: upgrade-project.sh prints 'Deployment: organizational' in the Upgrade Complete banner AND adds a 'last_upgrade' field, but .claude/phase-state.json .deployment is still 'personal'. The script writes last_upgrade but does NOT write the new deployment value. Downstream tooling that reads .deployment from phase-state.json will see the old value.

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Output enumerates next steps: fill Pre-Phase 0 organizational pre-conditions in APPROVAL_LOG.md, complete Section 8 of PROJECT_INTAKE.md (governance pre-flight), assign approval authorities for each phase gate.

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: After writing .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema, re-running upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational fails fast with '[FAIL] upgrade blocked — pending user decision' (PR #17 / U-F still fixed).

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: upgrade-project.sh header has both 'BL-006 (2026-04-24)' and 'BL-015 (2026-04-25)' changelog entries.

tests.name: no_template_placeholders_in_output
tests.details: grep of full upgrade output for __PROJECT_NAME__/__PLATFORM__/__LANGUAGE__/__TRACK__ returned 0 hits (U-I did not surface in this run).

framework_bugs.summary: U-G confirmation: intake-wizard.sh PROJECT_ROOT hardcoded to framework dir breaks --upgrade-deployment entirely
framework_bugs.expected: Wizard recognizes the project, exec's upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational, which then runs the upgrade.
framework_bugs.actual: Aborts with 'Error: PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found. Run this script from a Solo Orchestrator project directory.' before any flag-specific handling. Root cause at scripts/intake-wizard.sh:20 — PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)" resolves to the framework checkout regardless of cwd; INTAKE_FILE is then $FRAMEWORK/PROJECT_INTAKE.md (which legitimately doesn't exist). The runbook's prescribed Section F upgrade entry point is unusable for non-production targets.

framework_bugs.summary: U-J confirmation: upgrade-project.sh does not write new deployment value to .claude/phase-state.json
framework_bugs.reproduction: On a personal project with .deployment='personal', run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational from the project dir.
framework_bugs.actual: .deployment stays 'personal'; only .last_upgrade is added. The Upgrade Complete banner shows 'Deployment: organizational' even though the persisted state contradicts it.

documentation_gaps.gap: intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment help text says the value is 'personal|organizational', but for POC scenarios users are likely to type 'private_poc' or 'sponsored_poc' (the gov modes). The flag silently passes whatever value to upgrade-project.sh which then validates against personal|organizational only — confusing failure mode for users following the deployment-target naming.

Runbook Section F template uses TARGET_DEPLOYMENT placeholder without explicitly saying it should be 'organizational' for private_poc/sponsored_poc upgrades; combined with U-G this caused initial confusion.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Matches against password|api_key|secret|token in tracked files are all benign references (e.g. ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} in workflow, doc text in platform module / security-scan-guide.md). No actual credentials present.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log absent — no FORCE/reset events occurred during this run, consistent with the runbook's N/A guidance.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD still d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches setup-time SHA). git status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ paths from the surrounding agent session — neither was modified by this UAT run.

summary: Section A passed cleanly (PR #17/#18/#19 fixes hold). Baseline build (Phase 1 + Phase 2 with one recorded feature) succeeded. Section F: intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment is fully broken by U-G (PROJECT_ROOT hardcoded to framework dir) — High; using upgrade-project.sh as workaround the upgrade itself completed and BL-015 sentinel + U-F + U-E + U-H all stayed fixed, but U-J confirmed: phase-state.json .deployment is not updated. Section G clean.
